title: Problem with DISTINCT in sub-queries in a FROM clause.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INT, email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO users (name, age, email) VALUES ('Alice', 30, 'alice@example.com');
INSERT INTO users (name, age, email) VALUES ('Bob', NULL, 'bob@example.com');
SELECT DISTINCT name FROM (SELECT name FROM users WHERE age IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
SELECT DISTINCT age FROM (SELECT age FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%@example.com') AS subquery;
key_bug_features: DISTINCT in sub-queries in a FROM clause
